findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "search_pairs" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Discover supported crypto, equity, FX, and metal symbols before using the paid HTTP API. Returns up to 50 catalog matches with asset class, available services, …

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

description: Fetch one document or instrument guide returned by search. This is free, read-only content retrieval with no account, credential, payment, or live-price side effects.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "server" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_market_data_endpoint · Private data: get_market_data_endpoint, search, fetch · Public sink: get_market_data_endpoint.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_market_data_endpoint · Private data: get_market_data_endpoint, search, fetch · Public sink: get_market_data_endpoint

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
